an2.21:1.3: The one who doesn’t see his transgression as a transgression, and the one who doesn’t rightfully pardon another who has confessed his transgression.
an2.21:2.3: The one who sees his transgression as a transgression, and the one who rightfully pardons another who has confessed his transgression.
an2.23:1.3: He who explains what was not said or spoken by the Tathāgata as said or spoken by the Tathāgata. And he who explains what was said or spoken by the Tathāgata as not said or spoken by the Tathāgata.
an2.23:1.4: These are the two who slander the Tathāgata.”
an2.24:1.3: He who explains a discourse whose meaning needs to be inferred as one whose meaning has already been fully drawn out. And he who explains a discourse whose meaning has already been fully drawn out as one whose meaning needs to be inferred.
an2.24:1.4: These are the two who slander the Tathāgata.”
an2.31:1.1: “These two qualities have a share in clear knowing.
an2.31:1.4: “When tranquility is developed, what purpose does it serve?
an2.31:1.6: And when the mind is developed, what purpose does it serve?
an2.31:1.8: “When insight is developed, what purpose does it serve?
an2.31:1.10: And when discernment is developed, what purpose does it serve?
an2.31:1.12: “Defiled by passion, the mind is not released. Defiled by ignorance, discernment does not develop.
an2.31:1.13: Thus from the fading of passion is there awareness-release. From the fading of ignorance is there discernment-release.”
